contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 50
- Description: Made the GO convention real: Telegram adapter resolves thread_id → handoff plan (deployed)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/128
  
  Details:
  Fixed the keystone bug where a bare 'go for it' in a Telegram handoff topic had no referent (each message is a stateless turn, so Sophia replied generically). Root cause + fix: the thread_id already travels to the brain via build_session_id, and SOPHIA_HANDOFFS.md already maps thread_id → plan file — so wired them together in app/telegram_adapter.py: before dispatch, look up the current thread_id in the registry and, for an active handoff, prepend a context line instructing the brain to read that plan (read_context_file) and treat a go-signal as authorization to execute from RESUME HERE. Adapter-only and fail-safe (any lookup error dispatches the message unchanged → blast radius limited to the Telegram path). Split out a pure _parse_handoff_plan() + unit tests (thread-id match, session-id-suffix match, inactive-row ignored, unknown→None); verified against the live registry (1722→MORNING_ORACLE_STANDUP_PLAN, 1695→DAO_CLIENT_INTEGRATION_FIXES). Shipped truesight_autopilot PR #128, deployed to the box (git pull + systemctl restart truesight-autopilot-telegram.service, confirmed active). This makes the GO convention actually work end-to-end.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 83.33
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
